originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Big News from Djibouti: A Super Long-Serving President!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter, here to share some exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're zooming in on a small but very special country called Djibouti. It's a place with sunny skies and beautiful blue water.

Meet Mr. Guelleh, Djibouti's Leader

In Djibouti, there's a leader named Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. That's a big name, isn't it? He's 78 years old. That's older than most of your grandparents! Mr. Guelleh has been the President of Djibouti for a super, super long time. Can you guess how long? He has already been the president for 27 years!

A President's Big Job

Imagine being the class monitor for 27 years! Wow! Being a president is a very important job. A president is like the head of a big family, but for a whole country. They help make sure everyone is safe, that schools are good, and that people have jobs. It's a huge responsibility to look after all the people in a country.

Did You Know? What is a President?

Did you know that leaders around the world have different names? Some are called Presidents, like in Djibouti. Others are called Prime Ministers, and some countries even have Kings or Queens! No matter what they are called, their job is to lead and help their country.

Where is Djibouti?

Let's find Djibouti on the map! It's a small country on the East side of Africa, right next to the big blue ocean. It's a bit like a little gem on the map. It's super hot there, and you can see amazing things like beautiful salt lakes and even whale sharks in the sea! It's a special place because many ships stop there on their way across the ocean.

What is an Election?

Sometimes, people in a country get to choose who their leader will be. This is called an election. Everyone who is old enough gets to cast a vote. A vote is like saying 'I choose this person!' It's how people pick their president, like how you might pick your favorite toy or your class monitor.

The Big Vote in Djibouti!

Recently, Djibouti had an election. It was time for the people to choose their president again. And guess what? Mr. Guelleh won the election! Lots and lots of people voted for him. Almost everyone who voted chose him – like 97 out of every 100 people! That's a huge number of votes!

Sixth Time as President!

This means Mr. Guelleh will be president for even more years! This will be his sixth time being chosen as president. That's a lot of times, more than anyone else in Djibouti has ever been chosen. It's like winning the same big game six times in a row! This is very special because it means he has been chosen by the people for a very long period.

But Wait, What About Other Leaders?

Now, sometimes in an election, there are different people who want to be president. These are like other teams in a game. They are called 'opposition candidates.' But this time, some of the other people who wanted to be president said, 'We don't want to be part of this election.' They didn't think it was fair, so they decided not to participate.

What Does "Boycott" Mean?

When people decide not to take part in something because they don't think it's fair or right, we say they 'boycott' it. Imagine you are playing a game with your friends, but some of your friends think the rules are not fair. So, they decide not to play the game at all. That's a bit like what happened. The other people who wanted to be president felt the game wasn't fair, so they sat out.

More About Boycotting

So, because some of the other people who wanted to be president didn't participate, it meant that Mr. Guelleh was the only big candidate that many people could choose from. That's why he got almost all the votes. It's important for everyone to feel that elections are fair, so all different voices can be heard.

Did You Know? Lake Assal!

Did you know that Djibouti is home to one of the saltiest lakes in the world, called Lake Assal? It's even saltier than the ocean! It looks like a huge, sparkling white plain because of all the salt. You can even float really easily in it, just like in the Dead Sea!

Did You Know? Voting Age!

Did you know that the age to vote is different in some countries? In many places, like Djibouti, you need to be 18 years old to vote for your leaders. Voting is a very important way for people to have a say in how their country is run and who leads it.

What Happens Now for Djibouti?

So, Mr. Guelleh will continue to be the President of Djibouti. He will keep working hard for his country and its people. It's a big responsibility, making sure everyone in Djibouti has a good life, good schools, and a safe place to grow up.

Our African Story Continues!

That's all the news from Djibouti for today! It's a story about a leader who has served his country for a very long time and has been chosen again by his people. Keep learning and exploring the amazing world around us. See you next time, young explorers, for more news from our incredible continent, Africa!
summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has won his sixth election, continuing his very long time as President of Djibouti, even though some other leaders did not take part in the vote.
whyMatters: This is important because it means Mr. Guelleh will be the leader of Djibouti for even more years, guiding the country and its people, and it's a big event in how leaders are chosen.

originalTitle: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
title: Big News! Helping Keep Nigeria Safe!
content:
Hello, my super friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter for Africa, ready to share some important news from a very special country: Nigeria! Nigeria is a super big country in Africa, with lots and lots of wonderful people, amazing animals, and beautiful places. Just like your home, Nigeria needs everyone to be safe and happy. Sometimes, grown-ups called judges help make sure everyone is safe. Let's talk about what happened!

What Happened in Nigeria?

There were some people who caused trouble in Nigeria. These people made some communities feel scared and unsafe. They did things that broke important rules, like making noise when it should be quiet or hurting people instead of helping them. They caused a lot of worry for families and children. Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! That’s why keeping everyone safe there is so important.

Helping Hands: The Judges

When people break big rules and make others unsafe, special grown-ups called judges help. Judges work in a place called a court. Think of a court like a very big classroom where judges are like the teachers, making sure everyone follows the rules and being fair. Judges listen to what happened very carefully. They listen to everyone, like a referee in a game. Their job is to make sure that the rules are followed and that bad things don't keep happening. They want to make sure everyone can live peacefully.

What Judges Decided

Many, many people who caused trouble were looked at by these special judges. There were almost 400 of these people! The judges listened to all the stories and looked at all the facts. They made sure to be fair to everyone. After listening, the judges made a very big decision. They said that these people who caused trouble would need to go to a special place for a long time. Some will stay for many, many years, like five years. Some will stay for even longer, for their whole life. This is called a punishment, but it’s also to help keep everyone else safe. Did you know? Judges often wear special robes, like big gowns, when they are working in court. It helps everyone know they are important and make big decisions!

Why This Is Good News!

This news is really important for Nigeria, and for all of us! It means that the judges are working hard to make sure everyone is safe. When people who cause trouble are dealt with, it helps make communities peaceful again. Think about it like this: If someone keeps taking your toys, a grown-up steps in to stop them, right? This is similar. The judges are helping stop the trouble so children can play, go to school, and live without fear.

What Does 'Safe' Mean?

'Safe' means you are not in danger. It means you can play outside, go to school, and sleep soundly at night. When a community is safe, everyone can learn and grow and have fun. It's a very happy feeling! Did you know? Peace is when everyone can live together happily without fighting. It’s a wonderful gift we can all share!

Building a Better Future

When judges make decisions like this, it helps to build a better future for children in Nigeria. It shows that breaking rules has big consequences, and that people are working hard to protect good people. We all want to live in a world where everyone is kind and helpful. These judges are helping to make that happen. They are like superheroes who use rules and fairness to make things right.

How You Can Help Your Community Be Safe

Even kids like you can help make communities safe and happy! You can be kind to your friends. You can share your toys. You can listen to your teachers and parents. Following rules is very important for everyone. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 countries! Every country has its own rules and ways to keep people safe. By being good friends and good neighbours, we all help to create a peaceful world. So, let’s all try our best to be kind and follow the rules, just like the judges are helping Nigeria follow the big rules to keep everyone safe! Isn't it great to know that there are grown-ups working hard to keep everyone in Africa safe? We hope for more peace and happiness for all our friends in Nigeria and everywhere else! Thank you for listening to our news! See you next time!
summary: Judges in Nigeria have put nearly 400 people who caused trouble in communities into a special place for many years, helping to make everyone safer.
whyMatters: This is important because it means special grown-ups (judges) are working very hard to make sure children and families in Nigeria can be safe and happy, without fear from people who cause trouble.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

originalTitle: What happened in 40 days of war on Iran
title: News from Far Away: Understanding What Happened
content:
Hello everyone! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you important stories from our big, beautiful world. Today, we're going to talk about something that happened in a country far, far away from Africa. It's a place called Iran. Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries have very big disagreements. These disagreements can sometimes get very serious. For about 40 days, which is a little longer than one whole month, there were some problems in Iran. We're going to learn about what happened during this time, but in a very gentle way. We want to understand, but also remember that everyone wants to be safe and happy. It's like when friends have a disagreement, but much bigger and with more people involved. We want to know what happened to the people who live there.

Where is Iran?

Iran is a country on the continent of Asia. It's a very old place with lots of history and amazing stories! If you look at a big map of the world, you would find our home, Africa. Then, you would travel east, across some water, to find Iran. It has big mountains and also beautiful deserts. It's very different from many parts of Africa, but also beautiful in its own way. Imagine a place with amazing colourful carpets, delicious food, and friendly people. That's Iran! Did you know? Iran is home to some amazing animals, like the Persian leopard, which is a big, beautiful spotted cat! It's a bit like our own African leopard. Even though it's far away, we are all part of the same human family, and we care about each other.

What is a 'War' in Simple Words?

Sometimes, when countries have really big problems and can't agree, they might start fighting. We call this a "war." It's not like a small argument between children. It's very serious, and it can make many people sad and scared. During a war, things can get broken, and people can get hurt. No one really wins in a war because everyone loses something precious. It’s when grown-ups can't find a peaceful way to solve their problems, and instead, they use fighting. Everyone hopes that wars will stop quickly so that peace can come back and people can live normal lives again. Did you know? Many smart people in the world work very hard to help countries talk and solve problems without fighting! They are called diplomats, and they help people be friends.

Loud Noises and Things Flying

For those 40 days in Iran, there were "attacks." This means that loud noises were heard, and sometimes things were sent flying through the air. These things were not toys. They were made to cause damage to buildings and other places, like homes. Imagine very loud fireworks, but not for fun or celebration. These sounds can make children and grown-ups feel very scared. People would sometimes have to hide in safe places, like basements or special shelters, to be protected from the flying things. This happened in different parts of Iran, which meant many people felt unsafe for a little while. It's a very difficult time when you hear loud noises and worry about your home and your family's safety.

What Got Broken?

When those loud noises and flying things happened, they caused "damage." Damage means that things were broken or harmed. Imagine your favorite toy being broken, but much, much bigger. Like a big building or a whole street! Some buildings were hurt. This could be homes where families live, schools where children learn, or shops where people buy food. Roads and bridges also sometimes got broken, making it hard for people to travel to work or to get help. When buildings are damaged, people sometimes lose their homes. They have to leave everything behind and find somewhere new to stay. This is very sad because homes are special places where families feel safe and make happy memories together. Did you know? In many places around the world, like in Africa, people work together to rebuild after natural disasters, just like they would after damage from fighting. Teamwork makes things better!

People and Families Who Are Sad

The most important part of any story is about the people. In Iran, many people and families were caught in the middle of these problems. Children especially felt scared and confused. Imagine not being able to play outside because it's not safe, or not being able to go to school. Parents worried a lot about keeping their children safe and finding enough food and water for everyone in the family. Some people got hurt, and they needed kind doctors and nurses to help them get better and feel well again. It's very hard when you feel worried or sad for a long time. People need comfort, hugs, and kindness to feel better. Animals also get scared during these times! Pets like cats and dogs, and even wild animals, can be affected by the loud noises and danger too. Did you know? When you feel scared or sad, it's always good to talk to a grown-up you trust, like a parent or a teacher. They can help you feel safe and loved!

Helping Hands All Around

Even in hard times, there are always helpers! Many good people step forward to make things better and bring comfort. Doctors and nurses work very hard to care for people who are hurt or sick. They are truly heroes, helping everyone who needs it! Other kind people bring food, fresh water, and warm blankets to families who have lost their homes or don't have enough. Neighbours help neighbours. They share what they have and give comfort to each other, showing great kindness and courage. Big groups from all over the world also send help. They want to make sure everyone in Iran gets what they need to be safe and healthy. It shows us that even when things are difficult, people care about each other, no matter where they live in the world. Did you know? Many African countries have sent help to places around the world when people needed it, showing our big hearts and spirit of Ubuntu – I am because we are!

Why Peace is So Important

After all these difficult days, everyone hopes for one very important thing: peace. Peace means no more fighting, no more loud noises, and no more broken homes or schools. It means children can go back to school, play with their friends outside, and feel safe and happy in their beds every night. It means families can be together without worrying and start to rebuild their lives and their communities. Peace is like sunshine after a big storm. It makes everything feel bright, warm, and hopeful again for everyone. We all wish for peace for the children and families in Iran, and for everyone, everywhere in our beautiful world. Let's remember to be kind to each other and always try to solve our problems with words, not with fights. That's how we help make our world a peaceful place for everyone. Did you know? The United Nations is a big group of countries that tries to help everyone live in peace and be fair to each other. It's like a giant school for countries, teaching them how to get along!
summary: For about 40 days, people in Iran faced difficulties due to fighting, causing damage and making many feel sad, but helpers are working to bring peace back so everyone can be safe and happy.
whyMatters: It's important to know what happens in other parts of the world and to remember that everyone, especially children, deserves to live in safety and peace, which is why we hope for an end to all fighting.

originalTitle: Iran war: What is happening on day 44 of the US-Iran conflict?
title: Big Countries Try to Talk for Peace!
content:
Hello My Little Stars from Africa!

Hello, hello, hello, my wonderful friends! It's your favorite reporter here, bringing you news from all around our big, beautiful world! I hope you are all shining bright today, just like the sun over our African homes. Today, we have a story from very far away. It's about two big countries. Sometimes, countries are like big families, and they need to talk to each other to solve problems. That's what we are going to learn about today. Let’s jump right into our news adventure! We are going to learn about talking, and sharing, and trying to be friends, even when it's hard.

Two Big Countries and a Problem

Our story today is about two important countries. One is called the United States, or we can just say the US. The other country is called Iran. Now, sometimes, big countries, just like people, can have disagreements. It means they don't always agree on everything. When countries don't agree, it can cause problems. It's a bit like when you and your friend have different ideas about what game to play. Sometimes, these disagreements between countries can become very big. That's why talking is so important. Did you know? The United States is a country far, far away, across a big ocean! And Iran is another country, also far away from us, in a place called Asia. Our world is so big and full of different places!

What Are "Talks" Anyway?

So, what does it mean when we say "talks"? It means that people from these two countries sat down together. They wanted to chat. They wanted to listen to each other. It’s like when your teacher asks two friends who are arguing to sit down and talk about what happened. They try to find a way to make things better. They try to understand each other. This is a very important way to solve problems without getting even more upset. It is much better to talk than to shout or fight. Everyone learns more when they listen.

Meeting in a Faraway City: Islamabad

These important talks happened in a city called Islamabad. Islamabad is a very important city in another country called Pakistan. It's the capital city of Pakistan! Imagine a big, busy city where important people meet. That’s where the people from the US and Iran went. They traveled a long way to get there. They hoped that by meeting in a special, neutral place, they could find common ground. This city was chosen so that both countries could feel comfortable to speak their minds. It's a place where they hoped to build bridges, not walls. Did you know? A "capital city" is like the head office of a country! It's where the government leaders work and make big decisions for their country. Our own capital cities in Africa are very important too!

No Deal, Oh Dear!

Now, here's the part that makes us a little sad. The people from the US and Iran talked and talked. They spent many hours talking. They wanted to make a "deal." A deal is like a big promise or an agreement. It's like when you and your friend make a deal to share your toys. Everyone agrees on what to do. But after all their talking, they could not make a deal. The talks ended without any agreement. It means they still haven't found a way to work together or agree on things. It's like trying to build a tall tower with blocks, but some of the blocks don't fit together.

Blaming Each Other: Not So Friendly

And then, something else happened. When the talks ended without a deal, both countries started to blame each other. To "blame" someone means to say, "It's your fault!" The US said, "It's Iran's fault we couldn't make a deal." And Iran said, "No, it's the US's fault!" Oh dear! This is not a very friendly thing to do, is it? It's like when you and your friend can't decide whose fault it is when a game breaks. It's hard to solve problems when everyone is pointing fingers. It's much better to say, "Let's try again together." Did you know? When we blame each other, it can make problems bigger, not smaller. It's always better to focus on finding a solution together!

Why Do Countries Talk Anyway?

You might wonder, why do countries even bother to talk if it's so hard? Well, they talk because it's super important for peace! When countries have disagreements, it's called a "conflict." A conflict is like a big, serious argument. It's not a small fight in the playground. These big conflicts can make people very worried. They can make life difficult for many, many people. So, countries talk to try and stop these big conflicts. They talk to make sure everyone can live safely and happily. Talking is always the first step towards peace. It shows they are trying to understand each other, even when it's tough.

What Happens Next?

So, what happens now that the talks have ended without a deal? Well, even though they didn't make a deal this time, it doesn't mean they will stop trying forever. Maybe they will try again another day. Or maybe other countries, like good friends, will help them talk again. We hope they keep trying. It's like when you try to learn a new skill, like riding a bike. You might fall down a few times, but you don't give up! You keep trying until you succeed. That's what we hope for these countries too. We hope they find new ways to try and solve their problems. Did you know? Many countries in the world work together like a big team to help other countries solve problems! They want everyone to live in peace.

Our Hope for Peace in the World

Here in Africa, we know how important peace is. We want all countries, everywhere, to live in friendship. We wish for the US and Iran to find a way to talk again and make a deal. When countries are friends, it makes the whole world a happier and safer place for everyone, including all of us here. Every child deserves to grow up in a peaceful world, don't you agree? We can all hope and wish for peace. And remember, even in our own lives, talking kindly and listening to others is the best way to solve any problem!

Let's Keep Learning!

So, that's our news for today, my little explorers! We learned about talks, deals, and why it's important not to blame. Remember that even when things are hard, trying to talk is always the best way. Keep being kind, keep sharing, and keep learning new things every day! Your friendly African reporter will be back with more exciting news soon! Bye for now!
summary: Leaders from the United States and Iran met to talk and try to solve their problems, but they could not agree on a deal and blamed each other, making everyone hope they will try again for peace.
whyMatters: It's important because when big countries don't agree, it can make things hard and scary for many people, so we hope they can learn to be friends for a peaceful world.

originalTitle: 'Big punch in the face' - could Arsenal really blow title from here?
title: Arsenal's Big Football Race! Could They Still Win?
content:
Hello, Football Friends!

Hi everyone! It’s your friendly news reporter from Africa here, bringing you exciting news from the wonderful world of football! You know, that amazing game where teams kick a ball and try to score goals? It’s super popular, especially here in Africa! Lots of kids just like you love to play football in school or with their friends. It’s a game about teamwork, speed, and lots of fun! Today, we’re going to talk about a famous football team called Arsenal.

Who Are Arsenal?

Arsenal is a really big and old football team from a country far away called England. They wear bright red and white shirts. Many, many people all over the world cheer for Arsenal. Maybe you even know someone who likes Arsenal! They play in a very important football competition called the Premier League. It’s like the biggest football tournament in their country, where all the best teams play against each other to find out who is the champion! It's super exciting!

What is the Premier League Title?

Imagine you are playing a game at school, and you want your team to win the biggest trophy. Well, in football, winning the Premier League is like winning the biggest, most shiny trophy! It means your team is the *best* team in the whole competition for that year. Every team tries their very hardest to win this special title. It’s a very proud moment for the players and their fans when they win! They get to celebrate so much!

Arsenal Was Doing So Well!

Not long ago, Arsenal was doing super, super well! They were at the top of the Premier League table. Think of it like a giant leaderboard, showing which team has the most points. And Arsenal had lots and lots of points! They were what we call "nine points clear." What does that mean? It means they had nine more points than the team right below them. It’s like being way ahead in a race! If you are nine steps ahead of your friend in a race, you feel pretty good, right? Arsenal felt very good and their fans were smiling! **Did you know?** Football is played with a round ball, and you mostly use your feet! Only the goalkeeper can use their hands inside a special area to stop the ball!

How Do Teams Get Points?

Every time a team plays a game, they can get points. If they win a game, they get 3 points. If they draw (meaning both teams score the same number of goals, so it's a tie), they get 1 point. If they lose, they get 0 points. So, to be at the top, teams need to win many, many games! Arsenal had won so many games that they were nine points ahead of the next team. This was a really strong position to be in, like having a big head start!

Only a Few Games Left!

There were only six games left to play in the whole competition. Imagine, the finish line was almost in sight for Arsenal! Because Arsenal was so far ahead, many people thought, "Wow! Arsenal is definitely going to win the title this year!" It seemed almost certain. They just needed to keep playing well for a few more games, and the big trophy would be theirs. Everyone was very excited for them, shouting and cheering! **Did you know?** A standard football match lasts for 90 minutes, split into two halves of 45 minutes each! That's a lot of running and playing!

A "Big Punch in the Face"? What Does That Mean?

Now, here's where the news gets a bit surprising. The news article used a funny saying: "A big punch in the face." Does that mean someone actually got punched? No, no, don't worry! It’s just an expression. It means something *very disappointing* happened. It means something went wrong when everyone thought it would go right. It’s like when you are building a tall tower with blocks, and you are almost finished, and then suddenly it all falls down! It makes you feel sad and surprised.

Could Arsenal Really Lose?

So, the big question was: Even though Arsenal was so far ahead, could they still "blow the title"? "Blow the title" means to lose the championship even when you were in a very good position to win it. It means to let the big chance slip away. It's like having the finish line right in front of you, but then someone else runs past you at the last moment. People were wondering if Arsenal, after working so hard and being so far ahead, could somehow still not win the biggest prize. That would be a huge surprise! **Did you know?** The first rules of football were written in England in 1863! That's a very long time ago, even before your great-grandparents were born!

Why It Was a Big Deal

This was a big deal because it doesn't happen very often. Usually, if a team is nine points ahead with only six games left, they are almost certain winners. It takes a lot of hard work and good playing to get that far ahead. So, for people to even *think* about them losing was a big surprise! It made the football season very exciting and a little bit scary for Arsenal fans. They hoped their team would not let the big lead slip away. **Did you know?** The biggest football stadium in the world is in North Korea and can hold over 114,000 people! Imagine all those cheers for goals!

What We Learn from Football

Football, just like life, has its ups and downs. Sometimes you are winning, sometimes you are learning. What's important is to keep trying, keep working as a team, and never give up. Even if things get tough, or you face a "big punch in the face" moment, you can always learn and try your best next time. It shows us that in sports, nothing is certain until the very end! Keep pushing! This situation with Arsenal taught everyone that even when you are very good, you must always stay focused until the very, very end of the game. It reminded all the teams that every game counts, and every point is important. It was a big lesson about never giving up and always trying your best, no matter what! Keep playing, keep smiling, and keep learning! **Did you know?** Brazil is the only country to have won the FIFA World Cup five times! They are super football stars, like champions of champions!
summary: Arsenal, a famous football team, was far ahead in their league with only a few games left, but people wondered if they could still surprisingly lose the big championship trophy.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows that even when you are winning a lot, you must always try your best until the very end, and anything can happen in sports!

originalTitle: Fortune favours the brave - will Slot start Ngumoha against PSG?
title: Fortune favours the brave - will Slot start Ngumoha against PSG?
content: Liverpool's 17-year-old star Rio Ngumoha stole the show against Fulham - will Arne Slot now start him against PSG?
